title: Flaky Silky Indian Almond Halwa
description: Buttery, silky Indian dessert featuring almond extract, perfect for monsoon season. Diabetic-friendly and made with brine-wet almonds on a salt block.
instructions: Heat a salt block over medium heat.
Grind the almonds into a fine paste using a food processor.
Combine the almond paste, almond extract, ghee, and water in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and turns light golden brown.
Add the salt, cardamom powder, and sugar, and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the rose water and cook for 1 minute more.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool slightly.
Pour the halwa onto a greased plate and let it set at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Once set, cut the halwa into diamond-shaped pieces and serve warm or chilled.
Best served on a salt block, garnished with cardamom powder and a few almonds on top.
